facts: Panama are the only team out of all 48 sides at this tournament to finish without scoring a single goal.
Harry Kane's goal made him England's all-time World Cup top scorer with 11 goals, passing Gary Lineker's record of 10.
Kane and David Beckham are the only two Englishmen to score at three different editions of the tournament.
Ghana reached the knockout stage for the first time since 2010, when they famously came within a Luis Suárez handball of a semi-final.
Austria reached the knockout stage for the first time since 1982 — a 44-year wait.
Sasa Kalajdzic's 96th-minute equaliser was the latest goal to change a team's tournament fate in group-stage history, rescuing Austria from elimination with the last kick of the match.
Riyad Mahrez scored Algeria's go-ahead goal in the 93rd minute, only for Austria to respond two minutes later — both teams advanced and Iran were eliminated.
Lionel Messi became the first player in history to score in seven consecutive World Cup matches, a streak spanning from Australia in the 2022 round of 16 through all three 2026 group games.
Messi extended his own all-time World Cup scoring record to 19 goals, the most by any men's player in tournament history.
The previous record of six consecutive World Cup games with a goal was shared by Messi, Just Fontaine (1958), and Jairzinho (1970).
Davinson Sánchez had a 89th-minute header ruled out by VAR for offside by the narrowest of margins — described as 'the length of a toe' — denying Colombia a dramatic winner.
Colombia dominated with 24 shots to Portugal's 13 and an expected goals margin of 1.63 to 0.69, yet the match ended 0-0.
This is the first-ever Round of 32 match at this tournament — the opening game of the expanded knockout format.
Both nations are playing in a World Cup knockout match for the first time in their history.
South Africa and Canada have met only once before in international football — a 2-0 South Africa friendly win in 2007 — making this their first competitive encounter.
South Africa have never previously advanced past the group stage in any of their World Cup appearances, including as hosts in 2010.
This is Germany's first knockout match in 12 years — they were eliminated in the group stage at both 2018 and 2022. The last time they played a knockout game, they won the 2014 final.

Germany scored 10 goals in the group stage from seven different players, the most balanced attack at this tournament. Their 7-1 over Curaçao was the fourth time in history they scored seven in a tournament match — more than any team.
Germany have conceded in nine consecutive tournament matches, tying a team record set from 1934-1954.
Centre-back Nico Schlotterbeck is out for the tournament after injuring knee ligaments against Côte d'Ivoire; Antonio Rüdiger deputises.
Deniz Undav scored three goals without starting a single group match — the tournament's most prolific substitute.
Paraguay's Miguel Almirón is suspended after his red card against Türkiye. Coach Gustavo Alfaro's side posted the second-lowest xG of all 48 nations during the group stage.
Paraguay's last knockout appearance was 2010, when they beat Japan on penalties in the round of 16 before losing to Spain in the quarterfinals.
Julian Nagelsmann on Paraguay: 'They are an uncomfortable opponent for us. They defend well and they stay compact.'
This match falls exactly 32 years to the day since Netherlands beat Morocco 2-1 at USA '94, on June 29, 1994 — also in the group stage of a World Cup held in the United States.
Current Netherlands coach Ronald Koeman started that 1994 match against Morocco.
The Netherlands are three-time World Cup finalists (1974, 1978, 2010) but have never won the tournament.
Morocco reached the semi-finals in 2022, the best finish by an African nation in World Cup history.

Japan have never won a knockout match in tournament history, losing all four attempts: Turkey in 2002 (0-1), Paraguay in 2010 (penalties), Belgium in 2018 (2-3), and Croatia in 2022 (penalties). A win over Brazil would be a historic first.
Vinícius Jr scored in all three group matches — just the fifth Brazilian man to accomplish that at a single tournament edition.
Japan went unbeaten in Group F (W1 D2, 5 points), only the second time in their history they survived the group stage without a loss.

Japan will be without Takefusa Kubo (knee injury), with Daichi Kamada expected to rejoin the front three. Centre-back Kō Itakura faces a late fitness test after being forced off before half-time against Sweden.
Brazil are missing Raphinha through a fresh hamstring injury picked up in the group stage.
Japan stunned Brazil 3-2 in a friendly in October 2025 in Tokyo, coming from 2-0 down at half-time — their first-ever win over the five-time champions.

Their only previous tournament meeting was the 2006 group stage in Dortmund, where Brazil won 4-1 with a Ronaldo double. That Japan squad had only six players in European leagues; this one has just three domestic-based players.
Sweden's best tournament finish was runners-up in 1958 as hosts, but they have not won a knockout match since beating Switzerland in the 2018 round of 16.
France and Sweden have met once before at this stage of a major tournament — France won 2-0 in the Euro 2012 group stage.
Côte d'Ivoire are playing in a knockout match at this tournament for the first time in their history, having been eliminated in the group stage in all three previous appearances (2006, 2010, 2014).
Norway have not won a knockout match in tournament history — their only appearance in the knockout rounds was a 1-0 loss to Italy in the 1998 round of 16.

Mexico have been eliminated in the round of 16 in seven consecutive tournaments (1994-2022), a streak known as the 'fifth game curse.' This Round of 32 is a new format — but the pressure to finally break through to the later rounds remains.
Ecuador have never won a knockout match — their only appearance was a 1-0 loss to England in the 2006 round of 16.
Congo DR (as Zaire) appeared at the tournament once before in 1974 but were eliminated in the group stage, losing all three matches. This is their first-ever knockout match.
England have reached at least the round of 16 in six of their last seven tournament appearances.
Bosnia and Herzegovina are playing in a knockout match for the first time in their history — their only previous tournament appearance was a group-stage exit in 2014.

The USA's best knockout run was the 2002 quarterfinals, when they beat Mexico 2-0 in the round of 16 before losing to Germany. They were eliminated in the round of 16 in 2010 (Ghana), 2014 (Belgium), and 2022 (Netherlands).
Senegal reached the quarterfinals on their tournament debut in 2002 — including a famous 1-0 upset of holders France in the opening match — but have not won a knockout match since.
Croatia have reached the final in two of the last three tournaments (2018, 2022), winning four knockout matches in that run.

Portugal reached the semi-finals in 2006 (beating Netherlands and England en route) and the quarterfinals in 2022 (beating Switzerland 6-1), but have also fallen in the round of 16 in 2010 (Spain) and 2018 (Uruguay).
Austria have not won a knockout match since the 1954 tournament, when they beat hosts Switzerland 7-5 in the quarterfinals — the highest-scoring match in tournament history.
Spain have won 5 of their last 6 knockout matches at major tournaments.
Algeria have never won a knockout match — their only appearance was a 2-1 extra-time loss to Germany in the 2014 round of 16.

Switzerland have reached the knockout stage in four of the last five tournaments (2006, 2014, 2018, 2022) but have never won a knockout match at this tournament — losing in the round of 16 each time (Ukraine on penalties in 2006, Argentina in 2014, Sweden in 2018, Portugal 1-6 in 2022).
Cabo Verde are making their tournament debut and will be playing in a knockout match for the first time — one of the smallest nations (population ~600,000) ever to reach this stage.
Argentina have won 9 of their last 11 knockout matches at this tournament, including the entire 2022 title run.

Ghana have not won a knockout match since 2010, when they beat the USA 2-1 in extra time to reach the quarterfinals — where Asamoah Gyan's missed penalty after Luis Suárez's deliberate handball denied them a historic semi-final.
Colombia reached the quarterfinals in 2014 with James Rodriguez winning the Golden Boot, but were eliminated in the round of 16 in 2018.

Egypt are playing in a knockout match for the first time in their tournament history — despite being the most successful African nation in continental competition (7 Africa Cup of Nations titles), they have never advanced past the group stage.
Australia have never won a knockout match — losing to Italy (2006) and Argentina (2022) in the round of 16.
